title: Input Actions, Bindings, Focus, and Keyboard Equivalence · Program Brief: Action Abstraction
summary: Canvas clicks are the only navigation method, global key bindings fire inside text fields, focus becomes invisible, and repeat events race state changes. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the action abstraction increment in remappable action and focus-navigation map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.
Provide a usable minimum, scroll or alternate representation, and reversible recovery when the visual grid cannot fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 9897 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile named actions, keyboard equivalence, focus order, and binding scope; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes. A defensible solution applies Action Abstraction, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to remappable action and focus-navigation map.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_input_focus_bindings_4(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-action-abstraction-19ppwo
# Competency: maze-action-abstraction.
# Case 8789: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_action_abstraction_19ppwo(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_19ppwo = "19ppwo-owpp91-8789"
    assert evidence_variant_19ppwo
    evidence_axes_19ppwo = (
        "grid19ppwo",
        "topology8789",
        "searchowpp91",
        "event19ppwo8789",
        "access878919ppwo",
        "recoveryowpp918789",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_19ppwo)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Abstraction model · Theory Action Abstraction, keep Action Abstraction connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Abstraction model · Theory Action Abstraction, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Action Abstraction precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Abstraction model · Theory Action Abstraction, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3978 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile named actions, keyboard equivalence, focus order, and binding scope; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Abstraction model · Theory Action Abstraction, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Each device event should directly mutate maze state.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-action-abstraction-19ppwo(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_action_abstraction_19ppwo\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?keyboard_equivalents != actions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?ambiguous_bindings > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
